exports.content: C60 is only weakly bonded to perovskite layers, inducing high energetic disorder between perovskite and C60 that limits device performance at elevated operating temperatures, and a thin C60 layer is insufficient to protect against moisture or oxygen ingress
exports.label: c60_weak_bonding_problem

exports.content: The conduction band minimum (CBM) of 2D-RT films is closer to the CBM of C60 at the n-type contact, enabling more efficient charge transfer at the 2D/3D perovskite interface; 2D-TA films have higher CBM with less efficient charge transfer
exports.label: cbm_alignment_with_c60

exports.content: 2D-RT passivated encapsulated devices retain >95% of initial PCE (T95) after >1200 hours at damp-heat conditions (85C/85%RH), passing the IEC 61215:2016 industrial stability standard
exports.label: damp_heat_t95_over_1000h

exports.content: 2D-RT passivation minimizes device energy loss (Eg - qVoc) to 0.34 eV, representing ~96% of the thermodynamic Voc limit (1.262 V) for a bandgap of 1.55 eV, comparable to state-of-the-art GaAs solar cells at 98%
exports.label: energy_loss_0_34_ev

exports.content: Inverted (p-i-n) perovskite solar cells with glass/ITO/2PACz/3D perovskite/2D perovskite/C60/BCP/Ag architecture, using oleylammonium iodide (OLAI) post-treatment to form 2D perovskite passivation layers on the 3D perovskite surface at the electron-selective contact, encapsulated with vacuum-laminated glass/encapsulant and butyl rubber edge sealing for stability testing
exports.label: exp_context

exports.content: IEC 61215:2016 industrial standard requires photovoltaic modules to pass damp-heat testing at 85C and 85% relative humidity for >1000 hours with <5% relative loss in PCE, representing a critical benchmark for commercial viability
exports.label: iec_61215_standard

exports.content: How do 2D-RT passivated devices perform under real outdoor conditions with daily thermal cycling, UV exposure, and varying humidity beyond the 1000-hour damp-heat benchmark?
exports.label: long_term_outdoor_stability

exports.content: Encapsulated 2D-RT devices retain up to ~95% of initial PCE after >500 hours of maximum power point tracking under simulated 1-sun illumination in ambient air, while control devices retain <90% for only ~100 hours
exports.label: mppt_95_pct_500h

exports.content: Oleylammonium iodide (OLAI) molecules applied as post-treatment on 3D perovskite surfaces form Ruddlesden-Popper-phase 2D perovskite layers; the dimensionality (n) is controlled by annealing conditions: thermal annealing (2D-TA) produces n=1 dominated layers, while room-temperature processing (2D-RT) yields mixed n=1 and n=2 layers with more pronounced n=2 character
exports.label: olai_passivation

exports.content: 2D-RT passivated inverted PSCs achieve maximum PCE of 24.3% with stabilized PCE of ~24%, Voc=1.20 V, and FF=~82%, representing ~2% absolute PCE gain over unpassivated control devices
exports.label: pce_24_3_pct

exports.content: After >1000 hours of damp-heat testing, three devices show average PCE of 19.3+/-0.69%, representing a very high retained PCE compared to prior reports
exports.label: post_dh_pce_19_3_pct

exports.content: 2D-passivated devices exhibit longer charge recombination lifetime and lower ideality factor than control devices, confirming reduced trap-assisted recombination at 3D/C60 interfaces
exports.label: reduced_trap_recombination

exports.content: The 2D-RT passivation approach shows less than 0.5% deviation for person-to-person variations among seven different researchers, confirming high reproducibility
exports.label: reproducibility_high

exports.content: Room-temperature OLAI processing (2D-RT) forms higher-dimensionality 2D perovskite layers (n=1 and n=2 mixed, with more pronounced n=2) while thermal annealing (2D-TA) produces only n=1 layers; higher-n layers have lower formation energy and better electronic alignment with C60
exports.label: room_temp_higher_dimensionality

exports.content: Can the 2D-RT passivation approach be scaled to module-level fabrication using slot-die or blade-coating processes, and does the room-temperature processing advantage translate to industrial throughput?
exports.label: scale_up_2d_rt

exports.content: No substantial change in structural and optical properties of 2D perovskite passivation films (both 3D and 2D perovskites) after >500 hours of thermal annealing at 85C under dark conditions, confirming robustness of the 2D passivation approach
exports.label: structural_robustness_500h_85c

exports.content: The OLAI 2D-RT passivation approach is universal across various perovskite compositions (various bandgaps) and deposition techniques (one-step, two-step, blade-coating), yielding systematic absolute PCE enhancement of 1.5-2.0%
exports.label: universal_passivation
